title: Sequences & Series of Functions
description: Module 3 — theorem spine plus canonical examples and counterexamples

edges.label: in this mode of convergence, the index threshold \(N\) may depend on which variable?
edges.hint: The point is fixed first, and the required tail bound can vary from point to point.

edges.label: in this stronger mode, one \(N\) must work for how many points \(x\in E\)?
edges.hint: The same tail bound must hold across the entire domain.

edges.label: in the equivalent criterion, \(\sup_{x\in E}|f_n(x)-f(x)|\) tends to what value?
edges.hint: Uniform convergence is convergence in the sup norm.

edges.label: for each fixed \(x\), the real-number sequence whose limit defines \(f(x)\) is ___
edges.hint: Pointwise convergence first turns the problem into an ordinary sequence of real numbers.

edges.label: a uniform limit of continuous functions is automatically what?
edges.hint: This is the standard uniform limit theorem.

edges.label: on a compact interval, uniform convergence lets the limit and integral do what?
edges.hint: You can swap the order of the two operations.

edges.label: to conclude \(f_n'\to f'\), uniform convergence of \(f_n\) alone is what?
edges.hint: You also need control of the derivative sequence, plus convergence at a point.

edges.label: for each fixed \(x\), the object \(\sum f_n(x)\) becomes what familiar kind of object?
edges.hint: Fixing \(x\) turns each term into a real number.

edges.label: the truncation \(S_N(x)\) keeps terms only through which final index?
edges.hint: A partial sum stops at a finite cutoff.

edges.label: if \(|f_n(x)|\le M_n\) and \(\sum M_n\) converges, then \(\sum f_n\) converges ___ and absolutely.
edges.hint: This is exactly what the Weierstrass M-test gives you.

edges.label: in a power series, which shifted quantity is raised to the nth power?
edges.hint: The powers are measured relative to the center point.

edges.label: in \(\sum a_n(x-c)^n\), which symbol denotes the expansion point?
edges.hint: The powers are taken relative to this fixed point.

edges.label: when \(|x-c|\) is less than which constant?
edges.hint: This number sets the interior convergence region.

edges.label: at the endpoints \(|x-c|=R\), convergence must be checked how?
edges.hint: The radius alone does not decide boundary behavior.

edges.label: on every closed interval strictly inside \(|x-c|<R\), does one \(N\) work for all \(x\) in that interval?
edges.hint: Power-series convergence is locally uniform on compact subintervals.

edges.label: inside the interval of convergence, differentiating a power series term by term preserves the same radius of convergence: true or false?
edges.hint: Power series remain well behaved under termwise differentiation and integration.

edges.label: in the Taylor expansion, the coefficient of \((x-c)^n\) is \(f^{(n)}(c)\) divided by ___
edges.hint: The factorial normalizes the nth derivative term.

edges.label: the nth Taylor polynomial keeps terms only through degree ___
edges.hint: It is the finite truncation up to the nth-degree term.

edges.label: a Maclaurin expansion is the special Taylor case with center \(c=\) ___
edges.hint: Maclaurin means the expansion is centered at the origin.

edges.label: the quantity \(f(x)-T_n(x)\) measures the approximation ___
edges.hint: It is what remains after subtracting the polynomial approximation.

edges.label: Taylor's theorem gives a formula or bound for the approximation ___
edges.hint: The theorem quantifies how far the polynomial is from the function.

edges.label: if a function equals its Taylor series on a neighborhood, it has a local power-series ___
edges.hint: Analyticity means the function is genuinely given by a power series nearby.

edges.label: for \(f_n(x)=x^n\) on \([0,1]\), the required tail index depends strongly on how close \(x\) is to ___
edges.hint: Near \(x=1\), the powers decay very slowly, which blocks uniform convergence on the whole interval.

edges.label: for this example, the limit value on \([0,1)\) is ___.
edges.hint: This jump at \(x=1\) explains why convergence is not uniform on \([0,1]\).

edges.label: on \(|x|\le R\), choose \(M_n=R^n/n!\); then the \(e^x\) series converges ___.
edges.hint: The majorant \(\sum R^n/n!\) converges.

edges.label: for \(\ln(1+x)\) around 0, what is the radius of convergence?
edges.hint: Converges for \(|x|<1\), with endpoint behavior checked separately.

edges.label: all derivatives at 0 can vanish while \(f\neq\sum T_n\); smooth does NOT imply ___.
edges.hint: This is the standard real-analysis counterexample.
